title: lasiopetalum oldfieldii
intro: Lasiopetalum oldfieldii, also known as Oldfield's Horse-shoe Bush, is a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae, native to Australia. It has been traditionally used in Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, as well as treating skin conditions and wounds. Herbal preparations of Lasiopetalum oldfieldii are often made from its leaves and stems, which can be infused in tea or used in topical applications.
